content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 7, 1991


Lila Sills
Registrar of Voters Office
3700 Branch Center Rd., Suite A
Sacramento, CA  95827

RE:  Combined Campaign Report
     Our File No. I-91-285

Dear Ms. Sills:

     This is to confirm advice provided to your office on May 24,
1991, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/  This letter constitutes written advice
under Section 84205.

     Section 84205 states:

     The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this
title.

     A special election has been called for Assembly District 5.
The election date is July 23, 1991, and a run-off election is to
be held on September 17, 1991.  Because the period covered by the
second preelection statement due in connection with the July 23,
1991 election (Section 84200.8) extends beyond the closing date
for the semi-annual statement due for the first six months of 1991
(Section 84200), we believe it is appropriate for candidates being
voted on in that election, their controlled committees, and
committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in
that election to combine those two reports.

     Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on
the following schedule:


1/    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Lila Sills
Page Two

Type of Statement                Period Covered          Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election (for           Jan. 12/ - June 8       June 13
July election)

2nd Pre-Election                June 9 - July 6         July 11
(for July election)
Combined with semi-annual
statement for the first
half of 1991

Pre-run-off Election3/           July 7 - Aug. 31        September 5
(for Sept. election)

Semi-Annual                      Sept. 1 - Dec. 31       Jan. 31, 1992

     The contribution limitations imposed by Proposition 73 are
still in effect for special elections held to fill a vacant
elective office. A contributor may contribute up to the stated
limits (see enclosure) in the special election cycle, less any
amount the candidate transfers to his or her special election
committee bank account from any other campaign account which is
attributable to that contributor. In addition, contributors may
contribute up to the stated limits in the special runoff election
cycle to candidates being voted upon in the runoff election even
if they contributed the maximum amount in the special election
cycle.

     "Special election cycle" means the day on which the office
becomes vacant until the day of the special election.

     "Special runoff election cycle" means the day after the
special election until the day of the special runoff election.




_________________________
2/  The period covered by any statement begins on the day after
the closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no
previous statement has been filed.

3/  Candidates who lose the July 23, 1991 election and their
controlled committees are not required to file the pre-runoff
election statement due on September 5, 1991. Instead, such
candidates and committees will file a semi-annual statement
covering July 7 - December 31, 1991. This statement will be due
no later than January 31, 1992.

Lila Sills
Page Three

If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[illegible signature]
by: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

cc: Secretary of State
